predictions.source_quote: Our base case is for growth of around 2.5% in 2018, toward the high end of the range of 1.5%–2.5% experienced over the last three years.
The euro area is expected to perform strongly again in 2018, with GDP growth of close to 2%.
the Chinese economy is expected to grow by around 6%–6.5% next year
Our base case is for growth in the 1.5%–2.5% range.
Growth in emerging markets in aggregate is expected to be 4.9% in 2018, in line with a lower structural trend post-GFC.
an already tight labor market will grow tighter, driving the unemployment rate well below 4%
the Federal Reserve is increasingly likely to raise rates to 2% by the end of 2018, a more rapid pace than anticipated by the bond market.
our fair-value estimate for the benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yield remains centered near 2.5% in 2018
core inflation is likely to pick up gradually toward 1% in 2018.
